content.text:
You are updating a Smartresponsor Quality Atlas snapshot. Use only the provided repository facts and current snapshot. Do not invent tests, tools, or repo structure that are not evidenced. Keep scores within 0.0..10.0 and only override base metrics when the facts justify it. Return complete metric_updates for all base metrics. If evidence is insufficient for a metric, preserve the previous narrative in a minimally refreshed form rather than inventing new facts.

Component registry entry:
{
  "component_id": "documentating",
  "component_title": "Documentating",
  "github_repository": "local/documentating",
  "default_branch": "master",
  "enabled": true,
  "report_path": ".antora-src/modules/ROOT/pages/component/documentating/report/index.adoc",
  "local_path": ".",
  "notes": "Bootstrap-enabled in the atlas repository itself so the assessment workflow has a live local target."
}

Assessment cards:
[
  {
    "id": "repo_facts",
    "title": "Repository Facts",
    "purpose": "Collect deterministic repository facts before AI scoring.",
    "required_inputs": [
      "repository manifest",
      "component report path",
      "git metadata",
      "quality tool outputs"
    ],
    "required_outputs": [
      "facts bundle",
      "repo summary",
      "tool results"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "engineering_assessment",
    "title": "Engineering Assessment",
    "purpose": "Score the canonical engineering profile and capture narrative risks/improvements.",
    "required_inputs": [
      "metric catalog",
      "view profile engineering",
      "facts bundle",
      "previous snapshot if any"
    ],
    "required_outputs": [
      "per-axis scores",
      "metric deltas",
      "risks",
      "improvements",
      "tradeoffs"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "owner_assessment",
    "title": "Owner Assessment",
    "purpose": "Score ecosystem fit, RC momentum, and owner-facing summary.",
    "required_inputs": [
      "metric catalog",
      "view profile owner",
      "facts bundle",
      "previous snapshot if any"
    ],
    "required_outputs": [
      "owner summary",
      "top risks",
      "next actions",
      "overall RC verdict"
    ]
  },
  {
    "id": "adoc_render",
    "title": "Narrative Report Render",
    "purpose": "Render or refresh component adoc report from the structured snapshot.",
    "required_inputs": [
      "current snapshot",
      "component title",
      "report template context"
    ],
    "required_outputs": [
      "adoc rc summary",
      "scorecard block",
      "recommended next actions"
    ]
  }
]

Scoring model:
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "description": "Scoring and aggregation rules for Smartresponsor Quality Atlas.",
  "score_scale": {
    "min": 0.0,
    "max": 10.0,
    "display_min": 6.5,
    "display_max": 9.5
  },
  "maturity_scale": [
    {
      "id": "missing",
      "min_score": 0.0,
      "max_score": 2.49
    },
    {
      "id": "weak",
      "min_score": 2.5,
      "max_score": 4.99
    },
    {
      "id": "partial",
      "min_score": 5.0,
      "max_score": 7.49
    },
    {
      "id": "solid",
      "min_score": 7.5,
      "max_score": 8.74
    },
    {
      "id": "strong",
      "min_score": 8.75,
      "max_score": 10.0
    }
  ],
  "rules": {
    "irrelevant_metrics": "exclude_from_group_average",
    "group_aggregation": "weighted_average",
    "axis_aggregation": "weighted_average",
    "overall_aggregation": "profile_axis_average",
    "history_policy": "append_immutable_snapshot_then_refresh_current"
  }
}

Current snapshot metric context:
{
  "product": {
    "score": 7.8,
    "summary": "Antora-based static documentation site with a clear build/publish pipeline and custom domain, providing a solid identity for owner-facing docs.",
    "risks": [
      "Content quality gates are not enforced automatically in CI.",
      "Audience/scope for the docs is implicit rather than explicitly stated."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "State the target audience and scope on the site landing/README.",
      "Add pre-publish validation for content (e.g., link and structural checks)."
    ]
  },
  "architecture": {
    "score": 7.4,
    "summary": "Repository shows organized tooling and content boundaries (.gate, .deploy, .smoke), but there is little application architecture to assess and responsibilities are not documented.",
    "risks": [
      "Domain/bounded-context boundaries for repo tooling are not described.",
      "Coupling between scripts/workflows is not documented, which may hinder maintenance."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Add a brief architecture/ownership section mapping directories to responsibilities in README or BUILDING.",
      "Document how the Antora source, CI workflows, and publication artifacts relate."
    ]
  },
  "runtime": {
    "score": 7.1,
    "summary": "Static site runtime via GitHub Pages; scalability is largely handled by the platform, with no explicit runtime tuning or monitoring in the repo.",
    "risks": [
      "No runtime health checks or synthetic probes are wired into the publishing workflow.",
      "Edge/CDN configuration (e.g., Cloudflare) exists in repo but is not used in CI."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Document the hosting/edge strategy and expected caching behavior.",
      "Integrate a simple post-publish probe step to verify site availability.",
      "If Cloudflare is intended, add an automated deployment/configuration step."
    ]
  },
  "qa": {
    "score": 6.8,
    "summary": "Quality scaffolding exists (.gate, .smoke) but CI does not run automated tests or link/structure validation before publishing.",
    "risks": [
      "Broken links or content regressions could ship without detection.",
      "No contract/e2e checks exist for the published site artifacts."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Add link and content validation steps to the gh-pages workflow prior to publish.",
      "Wire .smoke probes or minimal checks to run on each build.",
      "Introduce a basic PR check that builds the site and surfaces issues early."
    ]
  },
  "ops": {
    "score": 7.7,
    "summary": "CI automates site build and gh-pages publish with concurrency control, and a scheduled assessment job maintains Quality Atlas snapshots.",
    "risks": [
      "Single production deploy on push without preview or staged environments.",
      "No explicit rollback mechanism is defined for gh-pages publishes."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Add PR previews or ephemeral deployments for review.",
      "Keep a last-known-good gh-pages reference and document rollback steps.",
      "Cache dependencies in CI to stabilize and speed up builds."
    ]
  },
  "market": {
    "score": 7.3,
    "summary": "As a documentation component, market alignment is secondary; the site emphasizes documentation quality and search via the Antora lunr extension.",
    "risks": [
      "Audience and value proposition are not clearly stated for external readers.",
      "No visible changelog/release notes to communicate documentation updates."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Add a short 'Who this is for' section on the landing page/README.",
      "Publish a simple docs changelog or release notes section in the site."
    ]
  },
  "overall": {
    "score": 7.5,
    "summary": "Solid automated documentation delivery with scheduled assessment support; primary gaps are automated content QA and staged delivery practices.",
    "risks": [
      "Potential to ship regressions due to lack of pre-publish checks.",
      "Operational assets remain unintegrated, creating drift between intent and execution."
    ],
    "improvements": [
      "Introduce CI quality gates and previews before publishing.",
      "Connect smoke/probes post-build and document ownership/rollback paths."
    ]
  }
}

    ".github/workflows/gh-pages.yml": "name: gh-pages\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches: [\"master\"]\n  workflow_dispatch: {}\n\npermissions:\n  contents: write\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: \"docs-gh-pages\"\n  cancel-in-progress: false\n\njobs:\n  deploy:\n    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'push' || !contains(github.event.head_commit.message, '[skip docs]') }}\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout master\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          ref: master\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Node\n        uses: actions/setup-node@v5\n        with:\n          node-version: \"24\"\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n        with:\n          python-version: \"3.12\"\n\n      - name: Install build dependencies\n        run: |\n          sudo apt-get update\n          sudo apt-get install -y pandoc\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          python -m pip install pyyaml\n          npm install\n\n      - name: Build canonical Antora source tree\n        run: |\n          python3 tools/build_antora_site.py\n\n      - name: Build site with Antora\n        run: |\n          npx antora antora-playbook.yml --fetch\n\n      - name: Copy public site extras\n        run: |\n          if [ -f docs/CNAME ]; then\n            cp docs/CNAME .site_build/CNAME\n          fi\n          if [ -d assets ]; then\n            mkdir -p .site_build/assets\n            cp -R assets/. .site_build/assets/\n          fi\n          touch .site_build/.nojekyll\n\n      - name: Publish owner-facing site to gh-pages\n        env:\n          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          set -euo pipefail\n          git config user.name \"github-actions[bot]\"\n          git config user.email \"github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com\"\n          git remote set-url origin \"https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git\"\n\n          workdir=\"$(mktemp -d)\"\n\n          if git ls-remote --exit-code origin gh-pages >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n            git fetch origin gh-pages:gh-pages\n            git worktree add --force \"$workdir\" gh-pages\n          else\n            git worktree add --detach \"$workdir\"\n            (\n              cd \"$workdir\"\n              git checkout --orphan gh-pages\n            )\n          fi\n\n          find \"$workdir\" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name '.git' -exec rm -rf {} +\n          cp -R .site_build/. \"$workdir\"/\n\n          (\n            cd \"$workdir\"\n            git add -A\n            if [ -n \"$(git status --porcelain)\" ]; then\n              git commit -m \"docs: publish canonical Antora site\"\n              git push origin HEAD:gh-pages --force\n            else\n              echo \"No site changes to publish.\"\n            fi\n          )\n\n          git worktree remove \"$workdir\" --force || true\n",
    ".github/workflows/quality-atlas-assessment.yml": "name: quality-atlas-assessment\n\non:\n  workflow_dispatch:\n    inputs:\n      component:\n        description: 'Optional component_id to assess (for example: documentating)'\n        required: false\n        type: string\n      mode:\n        description: 'Optional run mode override'\n        required: false\n        type: choice\n        options:\n          - auto\n          - dry-run\n          - responses\n        default: auto\n  schedule:\n    - cron: '27 9 */2 * *'\n\npermissions:\n  contents: write\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: quality-atlas-assessment\n  cancel-in-progress: false\n\njobs:\n  assess:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout master\n        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n        with:\n          ref: master\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.12'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          python -m pip install pyyaml\n\n      - name: Seed current component snapshots\n        run: |\n          python3 tools/seed_quality_atlas_snapshots.py\n\n      - name: Run scheduled assessment dry-run or AI-backed pass\n        id: assessment\n        env:\n          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}\n          QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL: ${{ vars.QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL || 'gpt-5' }}\n          INPUT_COMPONENT: ${{ github.event.inputs.component || '' }}\n          INPUT_MODE: ${{ github.event.inputs.mode || 'auto' }}\n        run: |\n          mode=\"dry-run\"\n          if [ \"${INPUT_MODE}\" = \"responses\" ]; then\n            mode=\"responses\"\n          elif [ \"${INPUT_MODE}\" = \"dry-run\" ]; then\n            mode=\"dry-run\"\n          elif [ -n \"${OPENAI_API_KEY}\" ]; then\n            mode=\"responses\"\n          fi\n\n          component_args=\"\"\n          selected_component=\"ALL_ENABLED\"\n          if [ -n \"${INPUT_COMPONENT}\" ]; then\n            component_args=\"--component ${INPUT_COMPONENT}\"\n            selected_component=\"${INPUT_COMPONENT}\"\n          fi\n\n          echo \"mode=${mode}\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n          echo \"component=${selected_component}\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n          echo \"Assessment mode=${mode} component=${selected_component}\"\n          python3 tools/run_quality_atlas_assessment.py --mode \"$mode\" ${component_args}\n      - name: Print assessment errors\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          python3 - <<'PY'\n          from pathlib import Path\n          import json\n          root = Path('.sync/quality-atlas/generated/assessment-runs')\n          if not root.exists():\n              print('No assessment run directory found.')\n              raise SystemExit(0)\n          runs = sorted([p for p in root.iterdir() if p.is_dir()])\n          if not runs:\n              print('No assessment runs found.')\n              raise SystemExit(0)\n          run_dir = runs[-1]\n          print(f'Latest assessment run: {run_dir}')\n          errors = sorted(run_dir.glob('*.error.json'))\n          if not errors:\n              print('No component error files found.')\n              raise SystemExit(0)\n          for path in errors:\n              print(f'\\n===== {path.name} =====')\n              try:\n                  payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))\n              except Exception:\n                  print(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'))\n                  continue\n              compact = {\n                  'component': payload.get('component'),\n                  'title': payload.get('title'),\n                  'mode': payload.get('mode'),\n                  'error_type': payload.get('error_type'),\n                  'error': payload.get('error'),\n              }\n              print(json.dumps(compact, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))\n          PY\n\n      - name: Write assessment step summary\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          python3 - <<'PY'\n          from pathlib import Path\n          import json\n          summary_path = Path('.sync/quality-atla"
  }
}

Response contract description:
{
  "version": "1.4",
  "description": "Strict response contract for AI-backed Quality Atlas assessment runs in Responses API structured-output mode.",
  "mode": "responses_json_schema",
  "base_metrics": [
    "product",
    "architecture",
    "runtime",
    "qa",
    "ops",
    "market",
    "overall"
  ],
  "response": {
    "component": {
      "type": "string",
      "required": true
    },
    "summary": {
      "type": "string",
      "required": true
    },
    "strengths": {
      "type": "string_array",
      "required": true
    },
    "risks": {
      "type": "string_array",
      "required": true
    },
    "next_actions": {
      "type": "string_array",
      "required": true
    },
    "notes": {
      "type": "string_array",
      "required": true
    },
    "confidence": {
      "type": "enum",
      "allowed": [
        "high",
        "medium",
        "low"
      ],
      "required": true
    },
    "evidence_paths": {
      "type": "string_array",
      "required": true
    },
    "suggested_score_overrides": {
      "type": "object",
      "key_space": "fixed_base_metrics",
      "allowed_keys": [
        "product",
        "architecture",
        "runtime",
        "qa",
        "ops",
        "market",
        "overall"
      ],
      "required": true,
      "required_keys": [
        "product",
        "architecture",
        "runtime",
        "qa",
        "ops",
        "market",
        "overall"
      ],
      "values": "float_0_to_10_or_null"
    },
    "metric_updates": {
      "type": "object",
      "allowed_keys": [
        "product",
        "architecture",
        "runtime",
        "qa",
        "ops",
        "market",
        "overall"
      ],
      "required": true,
      "required_keys": [
        "product",
        "architecture",
        "runtime",
        "qa",
        "ops",
        "market",
        "overall"
      ],
      "value_shape": {
        "summary": {
          "type": "string",
          "required": true
        },
        "risks": {
          "type": "string_array",
          "required": true
        },
        "improvements": {
          "type": "string_array",
          "required": true
        },
        "evidence": {
          "type": "string_array",
          "required": true
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Rules: risks/next_actions/strengths must be concise arrays of strings. metric_updates keys must be exactly product, architecture, runtime, qa, ops, market, overall. suggested_score_overrides may only include those keys. Every metric update must stay grounded in provided facts or current snapshot. Do not return markdown.
